dependencies: Reliance on insurance brokers and agents for distribution
Dependence on reinsurance counterparties for risk transfer
Reliance on third-party service providers for IT systems and operations
risks: Loss of business from major insurance brokers could materially affect business
Dependence on technology systems and cybersecurity
Concentration of business through major brokers (Marsh & McLennan represents 11% of gross premiums)
Reinsurance counterparty insolvency or non-payment risk
Operational systems failures including those of third parties
Concentration risk with Century Indemnity Company reinsurance recoverables

Sanctions compliance (Iran, Russia, other jurisdictions)
Anti-corruption laws
Reviver legislation allowing claims beyond statute of limitations (molestation cases)
Climate-related disclosure and regulatory requirements
ERISA compliance for pension plans
California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA)
Insurance regulatory requirements across multiple jurisdictions
Tax Information Exchange Agreements (TIEAs) requirements
Complex and varying insurance regulations across 54 countries and territories
Anti-money laundering laws
New York Department of Financial Services (NYDFS) Cybersecurity Regulation
Swiss corporate law changes and tax regulations
Brazil's Lei Geral de Protecao de Dados (LGPD)
Bermuda Monetary Authority (BMA) solvency and capital requirements
FDA and healthcare regulations for accident and health products
Insurance change of control laws requiring regulatory approval for 10%+ ownership
Bermuda corporate income tax effective January 1, 2025 at 15%
Lloyd's regulatory requirements in UK
Agricultural insurance regulations and USDA Risk Management Agency oversight
Solvency II regime in European Union
Anti-money laundering and financial crime regulations
OECD global minimum tax framework at 15%
Trade sanctions relating to certain countries
Risk-based capital (RBC) requirements in multiple jurisdictions
Data privacy and cybersecurity regulations
General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) in EU
Climate change disclosure requirements from multiple regulators
Terrorism Risk Insurance Program Reauthorization Act (TRIPRA) changes

U.S. state insurance department regulations and capital requirements
International Association of Insurance Supervisors (IAIS) ComFrame and International Capital Standard (ICS)
U.S. state insurance regulations and capital requirements
Swiss Financial Market Supervisory Authority (FINMA) oversight

U.S. Terrorism Risk Insurance Program Reauthorization Act (TRIPRA)
Changes in tax laws across operating jurisdictions

Potential sanctions from EU and OECD on countries not participating in tax information exchange
